meaning: adj.鸣不平的,发牢骚的,易怒的,暴躁的
examples: One gets unsettled, depressed, and inclined to be querulous.
The national academies are becoming ever more querulous and greedy, and require higher pay-offs each year.
Nothing was stirring except a brindled, grey cat, which crept from the ashes, and saluted me with a querulous mew.
Still, the querulous druggist and his crew managed to win third place in their class.
And then, a few querulous sessions later, I began to feel some pride, and not simply in my improved performance.
